title: Engineering the meme: the Cook Warriors™ operating model
excerpt: How VORVN turned an internet meme into Cook Warriors™ — and what 90 days of Search Console data say about what comes next.

body.text: Three years ago, "a frying pan with a sword for a handle" was a recurring internet joke — passed around Reddit, gaming forums and meme pages by people who kept saying the same thing: someone seriously needs to make this. Today, that joke ships from active stock via DHL Express to twelve-plus countries. It has a trademark. It has a manufacturing supply chain. It has a verified five-star reputation. And over the last 90 days alone, the words people typed into Google to find [Cook Warriors™](https://cookwarriors.com) generated 5,460 impressions and 560 clicks at an average position of 3.7 — a click-through rate of 10.3%, roughly three times the industry baseline.

This article is about how that happened. More importantly, it is about the operating model underneath, and why we believe it is the model that the next generation of independent, IP-owning brands will be built on.
The data behind the meme
From 21 February to 23 May 2026 — a single rolling quarter — Google Search Console reported the following for [cookwarriors.com](https://cookwarriors.com):
By the numbers

items: 3.7 average position in the results page.

Traffic across nine-plus countries, led by the United States (325 clicks), the United Kingdom (62), Canada (34), Germany (25), Australia (20), the Netherlands (12), Brazil (6), Indonesia (5) and Poland (5).

body.text: Two distinct spikes are visible in the curve — sharp, concentrated peaks in mid-April and mid-May. Those are not random. They are the fingerprints of content drops and campaign moments landing on an audience that was already waiting for them. The rest of the line — the steady daily baseline — is people typing variations of "sword frying pan", "[Eggscalibur™](https://eggscalibur.cookwarriors.com)", "Cook Warriors™", "anime cookware" and "meme frying pan" into Google and arriving exactly where we want them to arrive: on our own property, owned end to end.

The headline figure to read is not the volume of clicks. 560 clicks in a quarter is modest in absolute terms. What matters is the shape of that traffic. An average position of 3.7 with a CTR of 10.3% means that when people search for what we make, we are usually the answer — and they choose us over the alternative. That outcome cannot be bought in a media auction. It is a brand-and-IP result. We did not rent that traffic. We own the reason it exists.

It is also worth noting where the click-through rate is highest. The United Kingdom, Canada, Germany, Australia and the Netherlands are all running CTRs between 17% and 23% — well above the United States figure of 9.1%. That tells us the brand is doing two different things in two different geographies. In the United States, it is winning the cold discovery battle at the top of search. Elsewhere, it is winning recognition: people who already know the name are typing it in and clicking through. Both are healthy. Both are signal.
The VORVN operating model

VORVN is an independent IP and brand design house. We design, build and own digital-first brands out of Hong Kong, with our incubator studio in Bali. Cook Warriors™ is one of those brands. [Hearts Notes](/en/#portfolio) is another. [MAQTOB](/en/#portfolio) is the third currently in motion. The model is consistent across all of them:

items: We start from a validated demand signal — something the internet is already asking for, in its own words, before we have built anything.
We secure the IP early: trademark, domain, brand surface, channel ownership. Cook Warriors™ and Eggscalibur™ are registered marks held by VORVN Ltd.
We build the operational machine — manufacturing, logistics, customer support, content, analytics, email, infrastructure — in a way that we control end to end.
We retain the channel. Our customers buy from our store, on our domain, into our list, with our pixel, on our servers. Nothing in the relationship is rented.
We grow on real data, every day, against the audience we already have, not on a forecast we made in a slide deck.

body.text: This is what "autonomous" means in the VORVN positioning. Not autonomous from suppliers or partners — we work closely with both. Autonomous in the sense that the IP, the audience, the data and the channel sit with us. If a platform changes its algorithm tomorrow, we do not lose our business. We lose a marketing tactic. The business is downstream of what we own, not what we borrow.
Eggscalibur™ as a laboratory

[eggscalibur.cookwarriors.com](https://eggscalibur.cookwarriors.com) is not just a product page. It is a continuously running experiment. The subdomain hosts a constellation of pages — the meme origin page, the FAQ, the reviews wall, the worldwide buying guide, the legend and pop-culture comparison, the gift guides, the stainless steel deep-dive — each one designed to capture a specific intent that the internet has already shown us.

When Search Console surfaces a query trending toward the brand — "is Eggscalibur legit", "who makes Eggscalibur", "best cookware gifts 2026", "Excalibur frying pan", "sword handle frying pan" — we read it as a request. People are telling us, in their own language, what they need to see before they buy. We respond by building the page they were looking for. The result is a brand surface area that grows in the same direction the audience is already pulling.

This is what we mean when we say we populate the internet. Every page is a small piece of infrastructure, owned forever, that compounds. The 5,460 impressions in the last quarter are not a stock figure. They are the dividend of pages we built months and years earlier, still earning today.
Customer support as a growth function

Most companies treat customer support as a cost center. We treat it as a research function. Every DM that arrives in @cook.warriors on Instagram, every Trustpilot review, every email reply, every order note becomes a data point. When a customer in Poland wrote to us asking when the Katana edition of Eggscalibur™ would ship — and others echoed the same question — that single thread became a product roadmap decision. When a customer in the United States bought the pan as a wall piece after fending off an intruder with a regular skillet, that became a content angle. When a customer wrote that the pan was "shining like the first day" four months in, that became a copy block.

We grow one stitch at a time, in dialogue with the people who actually buy. There is no panel, no focus group, no synthetic persona. The audience tells us what to build next, in their own words, and we listen with discipline.
From a single iconic product to a category

Eggscalibur™ was always a beachhead, not a destination. The sword frying pan is the most visible expression of what Cook Warriors™ stands for — kitchen tools that look like they belong on a movie poster, engineered like real cookware — but the brand is being built to hold a much larger category. Three lanes are now active or in build:

items: Premium cookware. The Eggscalibur™ family expands beyond the 24 cm flagship pan into additional handle editions (the Katana is publicly teased), additional formats and the broader Cook Warriors™ catalog of iconic kitchen tools.

Learning experience and gifting. Cook Warriors™ is moving into the gift-economy and lifestyle layer, where the pan is one piece of a wider experience built around cooking as a moment, an identity and an act of giving.

Outfitters and apparel. The merch line — already live as a small range — is the next surface for the brand to live on outside the kitchen. T-shirts, accessories and collector pieces extend the IP into everyday wear.

body.text: Each lane is being built on the same foundation: owned channel, owned audience, owned IP, cookware-grade engineering, and customer-led iteration.
AI as the operating multiplier
None of this scales by hand at our size. The model only works because AI is integrated into every layer of how we run, deliberately and without theatre:

items: Search Console data is read, parsed and translated into content briefs that target the queries our audience is actually using, not the ones a keyword tool guesses they might.
Customer messages are routed, classified and summarized so that the human time we spend on support concentrates where it matters — relationships, edge cases, real conversations.
Email infrastructure runs on a self-hosted Listmonk stack with Amazon SES delivery, paired with AI-assisted segmentation so that every send respects the subscriber and earns its place in the inbox.

Analytics runs on a self-hosted Umami instance at [analytics.vorvn.com](https://analytics.vorvn.com), feeding session-level insight into landing-page iteration and funnel optimization without sending our customers' behavior to third parties.

Content production — pages, product descriptions, multilingual variants, social drops — uses AI as a leverage layer on top of a human editorial standard. Nothing ships without a human reading it last.

body.text: The result is that a small team can run a brand that looks and behaves like a much larger one, because the operational machine underneath is doing the work that, ten years ago, would have required a department.
What this means

Cook Warriors™ is one expression of the VORVN thesis. In 2026, the most defensible brands will be the ones that own their IP, own their channel, own their data and their customer relationship, and use AI to operate at a scale their headcount does not justify. The internet rewards the brands that show up, on their own surfaces, with the answer the audience was already looking for. It punishes the ones that rent everything they touch.

A meme is a free, public, unowned thing. A brand is what you get when you take that energy, give it a trademark, ship it, support it, and keep showing up — in search results, in DMs, in inboxes, on doorsteps, every day. Eggscalibur™ is the visible part. The operating model is the part we are most proud of.
We didn't invent the meme. We made it real — and we built the system that made it possible.

More from the same playbook is in motion across our [portfolio](/en/#portfolio). For licensing, partnerships, or simply to follow what comes next, [reach out](/en/contact) or watch this [newsroom](/en/newsroom).
